title: cedrus libani f. glauca
intro: Cedrus libani f. glauca, also known as the Lebanon cedar glaucous or Atlas cedar glaucous, is a variant of the Cedrus libani plant, a coniferous evergreen tree native to the mountainous regions of the Middle East. The essential oil extracted from the wood and needles of this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Cedrus libani f. glauca include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system, while also providing antioxidant and antiviral protection. In herbal preparations, the oil is often used in aromatherapy, topical applications, and as a component in perfumes, with the leaves and cones also used in traditional medicine and as a tea ingredient.
